data.title: Carvana shares 'due for a pause,' Q2 EBITDA could miss expectations - J.P. Morgan
data.description:
Carvana’s (NYSE:CVNA) meteoric 187% appreciation year-over-year is “due for a pause” after materially outperforming peers, with the car retailer facing larger-than-expected compressed gain-on-sale (“GoS”) margins based on recent prime deal disclosures, said J. P. Morgan analysts Rajat Gupta.

Fitch recently noted that CRVNA 2025-P2 auto receivables trust – which is backed by pools of prime auto loans – has a weighted average FICO score of 701, which is on the lower end relative to peer prime issuers. In addition, the offering’s percentage of extended-term loans of 61 or more months is elevated at 95.3% and loans with terms of more than 72 months comprised 47.9% of the pool, higher than most comparable transactions.

“It’s important to note that Carvana is not immune to recession risks but more importantly, [gain on sale] margins booked today already take into account ongoing loss performance across Carvana and industry with elevated return expectations baked in for residual tranche buyers,” Gupta said in Friday’s note to investors.  

Gupta also warns that the second quarter could be the first time in five quarters that the company’s EBITDA will miss Wall Street’s expectations. For the first quarter, Carvana (CNVA) reported record retail units sold, record profitability, and record net income margin.

Although Gupta maintained his Overweight rating on the stock, his cautious tone and prediction of a Q2 miss is weighing on Carvana (NYSE:CVNA) shares, last traded with a loss of 6% and its second day in negative territory.

data.title: Visa, Mastercard stocks slide after stablecoin report
data.description:
Mastercard (NYSE:MA) stock dropped 4.9% and Visa (NYSE:V) sank 5.3% in Friday late morning trading after a report said massive retailers Amazon.com (AMZN) and Walmart (WMT) are considering stablecoin payment systems that would bypass traditional payment rails.

Some fintechs also weakened, with PayPal Holdings (PYPL) wilting 4.4%, Paysafe (PSFE) dropping 3.8%, Shift4 Payments (FOUR) declining 3.7%, and Block (XYZ) dipping 2.1%.

The Israeli attack on Iran is likely contributing to investors' concerns about the economy, in general, and financial stocks, in particular. The S&P 500 Financials Sector Index was the largest decliner in Friday trading, down 1.5%. The Nasdaq Bank Index (BKX) fell 1.2%.

"A stagflationary growth lower/inflation higher shock from higher oil on top of Trump tariffs would further deteriorate the outlook and complicate the task of central bankers," Evercore ISI's Krishna Guha wrote in a note to clients.

Credit card names American Express (AXP) lost 2.1%, Bread Financial (BFH) -2.8%, and Synchrony Financial (SYF) -1.3%.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: Crypto-linked stocks, ADBE, V, MA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched lower on Friday, while oil prices surged amid escalating tensions in the Middle East.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • RH (NYSE:RH) +20% – Shares soared despite mixed FQ1 results, as the company laid out strategic plans to offset U.S. import tariffs. RH aims to reduce China sourcing from 16% to just 2%, with vendors absorbing a meaningful portion of the costs. The recently announced Liberation Day tariffs are expected to impact Q2 revenue by ~6 points, though the shortfall is expected to recover in H2. FQ2 revenue is forecasted at $896M–$913M, below the $916M estimate, with adjusted operating margin projected at 15%–16%. For FY2026, RH expects 10%–13% revenue growth ($3.5B–$3.6B), margins of 14%–15% (adjusted op.) and 20%–21% (EBITDA), and free cash flow of $250M–$350M. RH said it's confident navigating any market conditions despite lingering tariff uncertainty.
  • Celldex Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CLDX) +7% – Shares climbed after the company reported sustained efficacy data from its Phase 2 trial of barzolvolimab in treating chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU). Seven months post-dosing, 41% of patients continued to show complete response at the 76-week mark, while 48% reported their disease no longer impacted quality of life. The drug maintained a favorable safety profile, and HC Wainwright called the long-term results “impressive.”

Biggest stock losers

  • Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) -5% – Shares dipped even as the company raised its annual guidance following better-than-expected FQ2 results, driven by strong AI-fueled demand in its digital media segment. For Q3, Adobe guided adjusted EPS of $5.15–$5.20 (vs. $5.11 est.) and revenue of $5.875B–$5.925B (midpoint: $5.9B vs. $5.88B est.). For FY2025, Adobe now sees adjusted EPS of $20.50–$20.70 (vs. $20.36 est.) on revenue of $23.50B–$23.60B (vs. $23.45B est.), up from a prior EPS range of $20.20–$20.50 and revenue of $23.30B–$23.55B. Digital media revenue outlook was also raised to $17.45B–$17.50B from the prior view of $17.25B to $17.40B.
  • Hut 8 (NASDAQ:HUT) -3% – Crypto-linked stocks fell as Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and other major cryptocurrencies declined following Israel’s airstrikes on Iran, a major escalation in Middle East tensions. Bitcoin briefly dropped below $103,000 before paring losses, last trading around $105,041. Among other notable decliners, MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR), Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN), MARA Holdings (NASDAQ:MARA), CleanSpark (NASDAQ:CLSK), Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT), Hive Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE), and Bitfarms (NASDAQ:BITF) were all down over 2%.
  • Travel stocks under pressure — Heightened geopolitical tensions following Israel’s airstrike on Iran are weighing heavily on travel-related stocks, with airlines, cruise operators, and hotel names all trading lower. Airline stocks are leading the decline, especially carriers with significant international exposure—United Airlines (UAL), Delta Air Lines (DAL), and American Airlines (AAL) are each down 3% to 5%, while the U.S. Global Jets ETF (JETS) is off 3%. Cruise stocks are also under pressure, led by a ~5% drop in Carnival (CCL), whose Cunard line sails the Mediterranean and West Africa. Royal Caribbean (RCL), Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH), and Viking Holdings (VIK) are also trading defensively. Losses have extended to hotel stocks and online travel platforms, including Expedia (EXPE), Booking Holdings (BKNG), Tripadvisor (TRIP), Airbnb (ABNB), and Travelzoo (TZOO).
  • Visa (V) -6%, Mastercard (MA) -5%, and American Express (AXP) -2% – Payment stocks tumbled after a Wall Street Journal report revealed that major retailers are exploring stablecoin alternatives to bypass traditional card fees. Capital One (COF) dropped 3%, while PayPal (PYPL) and Block (SQ) slid 2.6% and 2.4%, respectively. Retail giants including Walmart (WMT), Amazon (AMZN), and Expedia (EXPE), along with several major airlines, are reportedly considering issuing their own stablecoins to slash billions in transaction costs by avoiding legacy payment networks. While currently used mostly in crypto trading, stablecoins, digital tokens pegged to fiat currencies, pose a growing threat to established payment systems. The rollout potential hinges on the Genius Act, a pending bill to regulate stablecoins, which still awaits full Congressional approval.

data.title: Bunge, CF Industries top S&P 500 as EPA proposes biofuel quota boost
data.description:
Bunge (NYSE:BG) and CF Industries (NYSE:CF) surge to the top of Friday's S&P 500 leaderboard, +6.1% and +5.6% respectively, after the Trump administration proposed to increase the amount of biofuels that oil refiners must blend into gasoline and diesel over the next two years, and included a surge in biomass-based diesel mandates.

Also, Darling Ingredients (DAR) +9.7%, Green Plains (GPRE) +4.3%, Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) +3.6%, Gevo (GEVO) +3.6%, REX American Resources (REX) +0.1%.

The big three oil refiner stocks trade mixed: Phillips 66 (PSX) +0.4%, Marathon Petroleum (MPC) -0.3%, Valero Energy (VLO) -0.6%.

The plan announced by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency would require total biofuel blending volumes at 24.02B gallons in 2026 and 24.46B gallons in 2027, compared to blending requirements of 22.33B gallons in 2025.

The EPA proposal also would set a 2026 mandate for biomass-based diesel typically made from soybeans and used cooking oil at 5.61M gallons, a 67% jump from the 3.35B gallons required this year.

CBOT soybean oil futures (SOYB) in Chicago climbed as much as 6.2%, the biggest increase in five months.

Ethanol and biodiesel producers cheered the plan, with the head of the Renewable Fuels Association Geoff Cooper saying the quotas would "provide crucial growth opportunities" for farmers and industry.

data.title: Israel PM Netanyahu talks to world leaders, due to speak with Trump, Putin, Starmer
data.description:
Israel's leader Benjamin Netanyahu has spoken to some world leaders since the country attacked Iran, and is due to have talks with U.S. President Donald Trump, Russia's Vladimir Putin and UK's Keir Starmer.

Israel late on Thursday hit Iran with a series of what it called "preemptive" strikes against nuclear sites and military targets. Oil prices (CL1:COM)(CO1:COM) have spiked in response, while U.S. stocks (SP500) have tumbled.

"Prime Minister Netanyahu, beginning last night, has held conversations with world leaders, including the German Chancellor, the Indian Prime Minister and the French President. He is due to speak with U.S. President Trump, Russian President Putin and the British Prime Minister," Netanyahu's official X (formerly Twitter) account said on Friday.

"The leaders showed understanding for Israel's defense needs in the face of the Iranian threat of annihilation; the Prime Minister said that he would continue to be in contact with them in the coming days," the account added.

U.S. President Trump earlier this morning said he had given Iran "chance after chance" to make a nuclear deal. "Iran must make a deal, before there is nothing left," Trump said on his Truth Social service. He later said in a separate post that Iran "perhaps" has a "second chance" to make a deal, without any further explanation.

France's President Emmanuel Macron on Friday said the European country had "repeatedly condemned Iran’s ongoing nuclear program" and had "taken all appropriate diplomatic measures in response."

"France stands ready to work with all its partners to push for de- escalation in the Near and Middle East," Macron said on X.

Meanwhile, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi confirmed that he had received a phone call from Netanyahu. 

"He briefed me on the evolving situation. I shared India's concerns and emphasized the need for early restoration of peace and stability in the region," Modi said on X. 

The S&P 500 Energy sector was the only one in positive territory on Friday, as oil prices surged.  

Here are some Israel-focused exchange-traded funds of interest: (NYSEARCA:EIS), (BATS:IZRL), (NYSEARCA:ISRA), and (NYSEARCA:ITEQ).

And here are some ETFs that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).  

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: GSK’s RSV vaccine to be reviewed by the EU for expanded use in adults
data.description:
• GSK plc (NYSE:GSK) said on Friday that the European Medicines Agency (EMA) has accepted its marketing application to expand the use of its respiratory syncytial virus vaccine to include adults from 18 years of age. 
  • European regulatory decision for the vaccine is expected in H1 2026.
  • GSK is continuing to seek expanded indications for its RSV vaccine in other geographies, including the US and Japan, the company added.

data.title: House passes bill to claw back $9.4B of prior appropriations
data.description:
The House voted 214-212 to pass a White House proposal to claw back $9.4B in previously approved federal funding, progressing in President Donald Trump's mission to cut back on federal spending and pull back on foreign aid.

The rescissions package cancels $8.3B of foreign aid and $1.1B of funding for Public Broadcasting. The move is an effort to formalize the cuts identified by the Department of Government Efficiency group that was led by Elon Musk. 

Four House Republicans — Mark Amodei (NV); Brian Fitzpatrick (PA), Nicole Malliotakis (NY), and Mike Turner (OH) — joined 208 Democrats in voting against the bill. Some Republicans, though, switched their "no" votes to "yes" when they saw it wouldn't otherwise pass.

Four Democrats and two Republicans missed the vote, NBC reported. Three Democratic-held seats are vacant due to deaths.

After the House passage, House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-LA) said the vote brings back "fiscal discipline" to Washington. "There will be more of this in the days to come," he said.

The measure goes to the Senate next, where it's not subject to the super-majority threshold of 60 votes. Thus, it can be passed with only Republican support. Republicans hold 53 seats in the Senate, Democrats hold 45, and independents hold two.

Republican senators Susan Collins of Maine and Lisa Murkowski of Alaska, though, have voiced concerns about the cuts. Collins is concerned about cutting funds to an HIV/AIDS program, and Murkowski objects to the cuts to the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.

Even if passed, the measures' $9.4B in savings is a tiny fraction of the estimated $2.4T cost of Trump's "One Big, Beautiful Bill."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: OpenAI CFO Friar says will continue working with Scale AI after deal: report
data.description:
• OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar said on Friday that the ChatGPT maker will continue working with Scale AI after Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) invested in the company, Reuters reported.
  • “We don't want to ice the ecosystem because acquisitions are going to happen,” Friar said at the VivaTech conference in Paris, according to the news outlet. “And if we ice each other out, I think we're actually going to slow the pace of innovation.”
  • OpenAI is financially backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), among others.
  • After much speculation, Meta confirmed late on Thursday it had invested $14.3B in Scale AI, taking a 49% stake. The investment valued the San Francisco-based company at over $29B.
  • A key aspect of the deal includes Scale CEO Alexandr Wang joining Meta to head its “superintelligence” team focused on artificial general intelligence.
  • Amid rumors of the deal, Seeking Alpha analysts said it could boost Meta's exposure to artificial intelligence and provide a “positive catalyst” for the Mark Zuckerberg-led company.

data.title: Short bets against energy stocks tick up in May
data.description:
Short sellers marginally increased their bets against oil and gas stocks in May with short interest in the energy sector reaching 2.65% compared to 2.57% in the month-ago period.

The short bets, where traders sell borrowed shares of companies intending to repurchase them later at a lower price, increased to 531.96M in May from 516.55M in April.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) rose 1.7% in May, compared to a 2.5% drop in the corresponding period in the previous year.

On May 31, 2025, OPEC+ announced a significant increase in oil production for July, adding 411,000 barrels per day. This decision, the third consecutive monthly increase, is part of a larger plan to increase production by 1.37M barrels per day by the end of 2026. 

The S&P 500 Energy sector (NYSEARCA:XLE) rose 0.9% in May, compared to a 5.5% gain in the S&P benchmark index (SP500).

Most shorted S&P 500 energy stocks as of May-end

  • APA Corporation (APA) 10.72% vs 10.41% in the prior month.
  • Texas Pacific Land (TPL) 6.80% vs 5.76%.
  • Schlumberger (SLB) 5.04% vs 4.63%.
  • Expand Energy (EXE) 4.95% vs 5.05%.
  • Valero Energy (VLO) 4.20% vs 3.94%.

Least shorted S&P 500 energy stocks as of May-end

  • Exxon Mobil (XOM) 1.01% vs 1.06% in the prior month.
  • The Williams Companies (WMB) 1.40% vs 1.62%.
  • ConocoPhillips (COP) 1.45% vs 1.61%.
  • Phillips 66 (PSX) 1.66% vs 3.37%.
  • ONEOK (OKE) 1.83% vs 1.65%.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

Oil & Gas Equipment & Services was the most shorted industry within the sector, with 3.90% short interest as of May-end, up from 3.55% during April-end.

 

Oil & Gas Midstream was the least shorted industry within the energy sector, with 1.85% short interest as of May-end, compared to 1.81% at the end of April.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow slide as Israel attacks Iran
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages slide on Friday after Israel targeted Iran with a series of strikes against nuclear sites and military targets, raising fears of an all-out war in the Middle East. 

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -1.5%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 4 basis points to 4.41%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 3.95%.

Iran also launched over 100 drones at Israel overnight in retaliation to Israel's "pre-emptive" strikes that targeted Iranian nuclear facilities and military sites late Thursday.

Israeli airspace has been closed, and a state of emergency has been declared as a counterattack was anticipated.

"The effects of the attack have cascaded across global markets, with a strong risk-off move for several asset classes," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Looking forward, the focus is now shifting to what form Iran’s retaliation might take. It’s also unclear whether talks between the U.S. and Iran over their nuclear program will continue, although AFP reported that the U.S. still wanted to hold talks this Sunday, Allen added.

"The scale of Israel’s air strikes against Iran was not anticipated by financial markets. As a result, the oil price has had the largest spike since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. The economic disruption may well be contained—the starting point was quite a weak oil market, given expectations of a U.S. growth slowdown," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Wall Street on Thursday took home some gains, as investors weighed a positive producer inflation report and a strong Treasury bond auction against geopolitical risks and trade tensions.

On the economic front, the consumer sentiment index rose to 60.5 in June, its first improvement in six months, from 52.2 in May, and exceeding the 53.5 consensus, according to data released by the University of Michigan on Friday.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and other major cryptocurrencies also fell in early Friday trading after Israel launched airstrikes on Iran.
